title: crepis vesicaria subsp. taraxacifolia
intro: Crepis vesicaria subsp. taraxacifolia, also known as hawk's beard or false dandelion, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Crepis vesicaria subsp. taraxacifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and support liver health, making it a popular herbal remedy. Crepis vesicaria subsp. taraxacifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
